focus: Retained in-process state behind iter-001: Project.close, registry caches, code-graph shutdown, embedder HTTP clients
noveltyJustification: Five new findings (F-011..F-015) plus two confirmations and one refinement of iter-001; cleanup-asymmetry between __init__ and close_all is new evidence not in iter-001.
summary: Project.close releases only the sqlite-vec ManagedConnection; ProjectRegistry leaks embedders + load-time events on close_all; system-code-graph shutdown never calls closeDb; LiteLLM embedder relies on upstream module-globals.

title: Project.close() only closes one sqlite handle; leaves coco.App + coco.Environment + embedder + sqlite-vec untouched
ProjectRegistry._embedder_by_config_hash never cleared by close_all() or remove_project()
system-code-graph SIGTERM/SIGINT shutdown never calls closeDb()
_refresh_project_if_config_changed partially evicts; leaks _load_time_done, _current_index_meta, and embedder cache
LiteLLMEmbedder constructed with no daemon-side close; relies on litellm module-globals
close_all walks _projects only, never iterates rerankers _ADAPTERS
F-005 should merge into F-010 — only daemon.py:378 is unreferenced; daemon.py:630-635 captures index_task

note: Cleanup-asymmetry table: ProjectRegistry.__init__ binds 7 caches (_projects, _index_locks, _load_time_done, _embedder, _embedder_by_config_hash, _project_effective_config_hash, _current_index_meta). close_all clears 3 (_projects, _index_locks, _load_time_done) + project.close on each value. remove_project pops 5 (no _embedder_by_config_hash). 2-3 caches leak per cleanup path.

Production callers of closeDb() in system-code-graph are limited to lib/recovery-procedures.ts:171 and lib/recovery-procedures.ts:262 (both destructive recovery paths). The SIGINT/SIGTERM handler in mcp_server/index.ts:64-69 closes only the IPC bridge. Tests and stress harnesses call closeDb but production shutdown does not.

core/project.py docstring at line 37 reads 'Close project resources to release file handles (LMDB, SQLite)' but the implementation only closes a single sqlite-vec ManagedConnection via env.get_context(SQLITE_DB). LMDB is not mentioned anywhere in this file — likely a stale docstring from a prior cocoindex backend.

hypothesis: embedders/registered_embedders.py holds runtime HTTP clients or model handles
reason: Read end-to-end (379 lines). Module is purely declarative @dataclass(frozen=True) metadata + tuple manifests + default_embedder() lookup. The default_embedder() call at line 378 runs at import time for validation but does not load model weights or open HTTP clients.

hypothesis: closeDb() is reachable from a normal production shutdown path
reason: Grep across system-code-graph/mcp_server/** shows production callers only in lib/recovery-procedures.ts:171 and lib/recovery-procedures.ts:262 (destructive recovery). shutdownCodeIndex (index.ts:53-62) does not invoke it. Tests and stress harnesses call it but those are not production paths.

hypothesis: system-code-graph handlers open per-call DB handles
reason: All read/write handlers (handlers/query.ts:205,353,882,1320,1404,1445,1471; lib/code-graph-context.ts:274,526; lib/cross-file-edge-resolver.ts:61; lib/ensure-ready.ts:368,554) call graphDb.getDb() which returns the singleton initialized in initDb. No per-call new Database() construction.
